mnemonics.title: malloc vs calloc
mnemonics.content:
MALLOC: Memory ALLOCate (raw, uninitialized)
CALLOC: CLEAR ALLOCate (zero-initialized)

Mnemonic: CALLOC = CLEAR + ALLOC = All elements start at ZERO

mnemonics.title: Memory Allocation Functions
mnemonics.content:
MRCF = Malloc, Realloc, Calloc, Free
Remember: Always CHECK for NULL after allocation, always FREE when done

mnemonics.title: Dynamic Array Creation Steps
mnemonics.content:
DECLARE pointer
ALLOCATE memory with malloc/calloc
CHECK for NULL
USE the array
FREE the memory
SET pointer to NULL

Mnemonic: D-A-C-U-F-S (Declare Allocate Check Use Free Set)

acronyms.usage: Allocates specified bytes of raw, uninitialized memory. Use: ptr = malloc(size);
Allocates zero-initialized memory for array. Use: ptr = calloc(n, size);
Resizes previously allocated memory block. Use: ptr = realloc(ptr, new_size);

acronyms.expansion: Noting Useful Link - or No Link
acronyms.usage: Pointer value indicating no valid memory address. Always check: if(ptr == NULL)
Technique of allocating memory at runtime using heap memory

visualTips.visualization: Draw a rectangle divided into two sections: BOTTOM = HEAP (grows upward, for dynamic data), TOP = STACK (grows downward, for function calls and local variables). Show arrows indicating growth directions.

Show consecutive memory blocks labeled with indices [0], [1], [2]... Each block is sizeof(type) bytes. The pointer holds the address of the first block. Arrows show how pointer+1 moves to next element.
Draw row pointers at top level, each pointing to a separate contiguous block of memory representing rows. This creates an array of arrays structure.
Draw a faucet (program) dripping water (memory) into a bucket (heap). When bucket fills without draining (no free), it overflows = crash. Show free() as opening the drain.

analogies.analogy: Think of hotel booking: STATIC = pre-booking all rooms for entire year (wasteful if unused), DYNAMIC = booking rooms only when guests arrive (efficient, pay for what you use).
Like checking into a hotel room and never checking out. The room stays marked occupied forever, eventually the hotel has no rooms left.

analogies.analoney: A pointer is like a house address written on paper. The paper (pointer) is not the house itself - it just tells you where the house (memory) is. Moving the paper doesn't move the house.

analogies.analogy: Like moving from a small apartment to a larger one. You find new space, move your belongings (data) to it, and give up the old space.
